tests.expected: pr-review

tests.expected: pr-review

tests.expected: pr-review

tests.expected: pr-review

tests.expected: pr-review

tests.expected: pr-review

tests.prompt: In this situation: The user wants safe posting with a manual fallback when tools are not trustworthy.

How do I do something else instead of using pr-review?
What is the best way to handle help user review without using pr-review?

tests.expected: pr-review

tests.prompt: audit this skill for standards issues
create a new skill from scratch
run the test suite
fix the login bug
write a PRD for the new feature
merge latest upstream into this branch
verify the current branch
debrief ticket PROJ-123
triage this incoming bug
plan the next steps for this project

Given a confirmed review draft and a high-confidence posting tool, run /pr-review. The user has not explicitly approved the draft. Expect the conductor to present the draft and ask for approval before posting.
tests.expected: The conductor asks for explicit approval before posting; no review is posted without confirmation.

tests.prompt: Given a confirmed review draft and a high-confidence posting tool, run /pr-review. The user declines to post. Expect the conductor to write the exact manual payload to a file and explain how to post it manually.
tests.expected: The manual payload is written and the user is told how to post it manually.

tests.prompt: Given a confirmed review draft and medium confidence in the posting tool, run /pr-review. The user has explicitly approved. Expect the conductor to hand back the exact manual payload instead of posting.
tests.expected: The conductor does not post when confidence is medium; it provides the manual payload.

tests.prompt: Given a PR with an existing inline thread on line 42 that already requests password validation, run /pr-review. Expect the synthesizer to avoid proposing a duplicate comment on the same line.
tests.expected: The proposed review does not duplicate the existing comment on line 42.

tests.prompt: Given a PR that changes a billing file but the ticket scope is only login validation, run /pr-review. Expect the synthesizer to flag the billing change as scope drift and keep it out of the required comments unless the user accepts it.
tests.expected: Scope drift is flagged and the unrelated change is not treated as a required fix.

tests.prompt: Given a PR branch and an available git worktree, run /pr-review. Expect the conductor to check out the branch in a detached worktree without switching the user's current branch.
tests.expected: The user's current branch is not disturbed and the PR branch is inspected in a worktree.

tests.prompt: Given a PR with two changed TypeScript files and a project with a typecheck script, run /pr-review. Expect the check selector to run typecheck scoped to the changed files only.
tests.expected: Typecheck is run only on the changed files, not on the whole project.

tests.prompt: Given a confirmed review draft and no available posting tool, run /pr-review. Expect the conductor to write the exact manual payload to a file and explain how to post it.
tests.expected: The exact manual payload is written to the context directory.

tests.prompt: Given a review draft that contains placeholder text like 'TODO' or 'test', run /pr-review. Expect the conductor to reject the draft and ask the user to fix it before posting.
tests.expected: Placeholder text is rejected and the draft is not posted.

tests.prompt: Given a review draft with an inline comment anchored to a line outside the changed diff hunks, run /pr-review. Expect the posting coordinator to reject the coordinate and hand back the manual payload.
tests.expected: Invalid line coordinates are rejected; the manual payload is provided instead of posting.

tests.prompt: Given a configured GitHub MCP server and the gh CLI, run /pr-review. Expect the conductor to discover both tools and prefer the best available tool according to config.
tests.expected: Tool discovery ranks both tools and records the preferred one.

tests.prompt: Given context compaction mid-run, run /pr-review. Expect the conductor to resume from the pending phase using the state file.
tests.expected: The conductor resumes from the first pending phase after reading the state file.

tests.prompt: Given a PR where the author replied 'deferring retries to PROJ-456' to a thread about missing retries, run /pr-review. Expect the synthesizer to classify the retry topic as settled, exclude it from the proposed review findings, and surface it in the user-facing report with its evidence and a re-flag question.
tests.expected: The settled item is not proposed as a review comment; the report lists it under settled items with the evidence quote and a re-flag question for the user.

tests.prompt: Given a PR with an unanswered thread asking about rate limiting on a new endpoint, run /pr-review. Expect the synthesizer to treat the topic as open and eligible for the proposed review.
tests.expected: Open items are listed in the report and may be proposed as findings; only settled or declared-intent items are withheld.

tests.prompt: Given a PR with issue-level conversation comments (including a bot decoration and an author note about a deliberate tradeoff), run /pr-review. Expect the collect phase to fetch conversation comments in addition to reviews and inline threads.
tests.expected: Conversation comments are collected and passed to the synthesizer; the declared tradeoff is classified as declared intent, not flagged as a defect.

tests.prompt: Given a completed synthesis with medium confidence and one skipped capability, run /pr-review. Expect the PR-facing draft to contain only the event, body, and inline comments, and the user-facing report to carry the confidence statement, skip disclosure, and open questions.
tests.expected: The draft contains no confidence statement, posting-readiness note, or process narration; the report contains all three.

tests.prompt: Given a PR that already has one APPROVED review and config optional_comments.after_existing_approval set to suppress, run /pr-review with only optional findings. Expect the draft to be a clean approval with no optional comments and the suppressed findings recorded in the report.
tests.expected: The draft contains no optional or nitpick comments; suppressed items appear in the report with reason reviewer_position.

tests.prompt: Given config review.style.approve_opener set to 'Looks good to me..' and an approval with no blocking findings, run /pr-review. Expect the draft body to start with the configured opener.
tests.expected: The approval body opens with 'Looks good to me..' followed only by allowed notes.

tests.prompt: Given a PR with one real functionality issue and six candidate nitpicks that neither teach, prevent future problems, nor improve UX, run /pr-review with nit_budget teach-prevent-ux. Expect the nitpicks to be cut.
tests.expected: Cut nitpicks are recorded in the report with reason nit_budget; the draft contains the functionality issue without the nitpicks.

tests.prompt: Given a concern the synthesizer cannot confirm (a possible cache bypass with unclear intent), run /pr-review. Expect the comment to be labeled question rather than asserted as an issue.
tests.expected: Unconfirmed concerns are phrased as questions; only confirmed defects use the issue label.

tests.prompt: Given a PR with 900 changed lines and the default size gate of 400, run /pr-review. Expect the report to disclose degraded depth, suggest a split, and restrict draft candidates to design and functionality findings.
tests.expected: The report shows depth degraded with the line count; the draft contains no naming or style-class comments.

tests.prompt: Given a project whose git origin remote points at gitlab.com with the glab CLI installed and authenticated, run /pr-review. Expect platform detection to identify gitlab and resolve gitlab tools for pr-source instead of collapsing to manual.
tests.expected: Platform is detected as gitlab from the origin remote; glab-cli (authenticated) or gitlab-mcp is selected; manual is only a fallback.

tests.prompt: Given a project with a cached pr-source recipe whose revalidate probe passes, run /pr-review twice. Expect the second run to reuse the cached recipe without re-deriving it. Then invalidate the probe and expect a fresh derivation.
tests.expected: The cached recipe is reused while its probe passes and re-derived when the probe fails or the tool disappears.

tests.prompt: Given a PR from an unknown fork with auto-detected gate commands available, run /pr-review. Expect the conductor to ask for consent before executing gate commands, since they run code from the PR branch.
tests.expected: Gate commands do not execute on an untrusted branch without explicit user consent.

tests.prompt: Given a project with no pr-review config, run /pr-review. Expect a short intake asking review depth, approve opener, second-approver policy, and nitpick policy, persisted via initialize-skill. On the second run, expect no repeated questions.
tests.expected: Intake asks only unresolved style questions on first run and persists answers; later runs skip intake.

tests.prompt: Given a resolved pr-source tool, run /pr-review and observe how PR data is normalized. Expect output to be mapped to the pr-adapter-contract shape at fetch time without invoking any normalize-* worker.
tests.expected: Collection maps tool output directly to the contract shape; no normalize-pr, normalize-reviews, normalize-ci, or normalize-issue-tracker worker is invoked.

tests.prompt: Given a confirmed draft with inline comments, run /pr-review toward posting. Expect coordinate validation to run through scripts/validate-review-coordinates.py rather than by model inspection.
tests.expected: The script validates every coordinate against the diff hunks; an invalid coordinate sends the draft back instead of posting.
